rows.title: Tab Orientation
rows.business_question: What is this tab for, and which decision should I make here?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Orient business users on current spend, return, and where to investigate next.
rows.why_this_exists: What is this tab for, and which decision should I make here?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Landing context for the tab, including headline and action orientation.
rows.formula: No arithmetic KPI. This section is contextual and directional.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: context and selected scope are clearly aligned. Risk: users act on wrong product/year context.
rows.decision_supported: Should I continue analysis in this tab or switch to another module?
rows.recommended_next_action: Verify selected context, then use the main KPI/graph below for decisioning.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Treating overview text as evidence rather than using quantitative sections.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Depends on selected model version and available artifacts.

rows.title: Topline KPI Row
rows.business_question: Do spend and incremental outcomes justify action now?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Orient business users on current spend, return, and where to investigate next.
rows.why_this_exists: Are we spending efficiently and creating enough incremental sales?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Topline scorecards summarizing spend, incremental sales, efficiency, and model quality.
rows.formula: Total Spend = Σ Spend_i; Incremental Sales = Σ Incremental_i; ROI = Incremental Sales / Total Spend; R² = 1 - SSE/SST.
rows.unit_and_scale: Currency and ratio (x), plus unitless R² [0..1].
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: ROI improving with stable/strong R². Risk: high ROI but weak model quality or unstable decomposition.
rows.decision_supported: Should leadership trust this plan enough to move budget now?
rows.recommended_next_action: If KPI signal is mixed, inspect Sales Impact and Insights before reallocating.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Comparing KPI values across different products/currencies without normalization.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Sensitive to selected year/version and may shift with new model runs.
rows.worked_example: Example: if total incremental = 3.2M and total spend = 1.6M, ROI = 3.2/1.6 = 2.0x.

rows.title: Quick Actions
rows.business_question: Which module should I open next to answer the current planning question?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Orient business users on current spend, return, and where to investigate next.
rows.why_this_exists: Which module should I open next to answer the current planning question?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Action shortcuts and recent scenarios to reduce time-to-decision.
rows.formula: No direct formula. This is workflow control.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: scenario list reflects current planning cadence. Risk: stale scenario selected by mistake.
rows.decision_supported: Where to branch next in the analysis flow.
rows.recommended_next_action: Open the module matching your current decision intent (impact, curve, allocation, simulation).
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Assuming scenario recency equals scenario quality.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Scenario list depends on saved state and may include exploratory drafts.

rows.title: Monthly Incremental Trend
rows.business_question: When in the year did incremental sales accelerate or stall?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Orient business users on current spend, return, and where to investigate next.
rows.why_this_exists: When in the year did incremental sales accelerate or stall?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Time-series of incremental contribution by month.
rows.formula: Monthly Incremental_t = Σ Incremental_{i,t} across channels i for month t.
rows.unit_and_scale: Currency or volume proxy, monthly granularity.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: predictable peaks align with campaign timing. Risk: unexplained volatility or declining trend.
rows.decision_supported: Whether to shift spend timing and campaign cadence.
rows.recommended_next_action: Investigate channel-level contributions for abnormal months.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Confusing seasonal base effects with media-driven uplift.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Monthly aggregation can hide intra-month campaign dynamics.

rows.title: Channel Mix
rows.business_question: Which channels currently create the largest share of incremental impact?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Orient business users on current spend, return, and where to investigate next.
rows.why_this_exists: Which channels currently create the largest share of incremental impact?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Distribution of incremental effect across channels.
rows.formula: Share_i = Incremental_i / Σ Incremental_j.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: effect share is balanced with strategic intent. Risk: over-concentration in one channel.
rows.decision_supported: Whether to rebalance channel mix or protect strong performers.
rows.recommended_next_action: Use Response Curves to confirm headroom before scaling top-share channels.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Assuming highest share channel always has best marginal return.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Shares depend on model decomposition assumptions.

rows.title: Cross-Product ROAS
rows.business_question: How does this product’s efficiency compare with peers?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Orient business users on current spend, return, and where to investigate next.
rows.why_this_exists: How does this product’s efficiency compare with peers?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Portfolio-level ROAS comparison across products.
rows.formula: ROAS_product = Incremental_product / Spend_product.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: target product competitive or improving vs peers. Risk: persistent underperformance.
rows.decision_supported: Portfolio prioritization across brands/products.
rows.recommended_next_action: If lagging peers, run allocator and scenario simulations for recovery options.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Comparing products with materially different lifecycle stages without context.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Cross-product comparability depends on consistent model calibration.

rows.title: Model Credibility KPIs
rows.business_question: Is the model quality adequate for planning decisions?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Validate model credibility and understand which channels are truly moving sales.
rows.why_this_exists: Are we spending efficiently and creating enough incremental sales?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Topline scorecards summarizing spend, incremental sales, efficiency, and model quality.
rows.formula: Total Spend = Σ Spend_i; Incremental Sales = Σ Incremental_i; ROI = Incremental Sales / Total Spend; R² = 1 - SSE/SST.
rows.unit_and_scale: Currency and ratio (x), plus unitless R² [0..1].
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: ROI improving with stable/strong R². Risk: high ROI but weak model quality or unstable decomposition.
rows.decision_supported: Should leadership trust this plan enough to move budget now?
rows.recommended_next_action: If KPI signal is mixed, inspect Sales Impact and Insights before reallocating.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Comparing KPI values across different products/currencies without normalization.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Sensitive to selected year/version and may shift with new model runs.
rows.worked_example: Example: if SSE=120 and SST=400, R² = 1 - 120/400 = 0.70.

rows.title: Annual Volume Impact
rows.business_question: Which channels contributed most each month in relative terms?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Validate model credibility and understand which channels are truly moving sales.
rows.why_this_exists: Which channels contributed most each month in relative terms?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Stacked percent chart of monthly channel impact composition.
rows.formula: Monthly Share_{i,t} = Incremental_{i,t} / Σ_k Incremental_{k,t}.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: composition aligns with strategic channel roles. Risk: sudden unexplained channel dominance shifts.
rows.decision_supported: Whether channel role mix changed and needs budget correction.
rows.recommended_next_action: Investigate months with abrupt share shifts using channel absolute contributions.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Reading percent stack without absolute volume context.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Percent view can hide months with low absolute impact.

rows.title: Channel Contributions
rows.business_question: What is the absolute incremental impact by channel over time?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Validate model credibility and understand which channels are truly moving sales.
rows.why_this_exists: What is the absolute incremental impact by channel over time?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Absolute stacked area of channel incremental outcomes.
rows.formula: Incremental_{i,t} from decomposition, aggregated by channel i and time t.
rows.unit_and_scale: Currency or response units, absolute scale.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: high-impact channels stable and explainable. Risk: volatility without business explanation.
rows.decision_supported: Which channels should be defended or challenged in planning.
rows.recommended_next_action: Use Response Curves to verify if top absolute contributors still have marginal headroom.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Confusing large absolute impact with high efficiency.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Dependent on decomposition quality and model version.

rows.title: Next Step Prompt
rows.business_question: What is the recommended next workflow step?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Validate model credibility and understand which channels are truly moving sales.
rows.why_this_exists: What is the recommended next workflow step?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Contextual CTA that routes users to the next analytical action.
rows.formula: No direct formula.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: prompt aligns with unresolved decision. Risk: skipping intermediate validation tabs.
rows.decision_supported: Which module to open next.
rows.recommended_next_action: Follow prompt only after validating current tab’s key evidence.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Treating prompt as a recommendation engine.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Prompt is static workflow guidance, not dynamic optimization.

rows.title: Primary Response Curves
rows.business_question: Where is additional spend still productive?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Evaluate diminishing returns and decide where incremental budget still has headroom.
rows.why_this_exists: How much incremental outcome is expected as spend increases?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Spend-response relationship by channel with current spend marker.
rows.formula: Saturation(x)=x^α/(x^α+λ^α); Response(x)=Scale×Saturation(x).
rows.unit_and_scale: X: currency spend; Y: incremental response (currency/units).
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: selected channels show steep slope near current spend. Risk: flat slope indicates saturation.
rows.decision_supported: Where incremental budget is likely to return most.
rows.recommended_next_action: Shift marginal budget toward steeper curves, then validate in allocator.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Treating long-run curve extrapolation as guaranteed realized outcome.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Curve uncertainty and calibration depend on model artifacts.
rows.worked_example: Example: if spend moves from 100k to 120k and response from 240k to 268k, local mROI ≈ (268-240)/(120-100)=1.4x.

rows.title: Per-Channel Metric Cards
rows.business_question: What are current spend, response, and efficiency signals per channel?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Evaluate diminishing returns and decide where incremental budget still has headroom.
rows.why_this_exists: What are current spend, response, and efficiency signals per channel?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Compact channel-level metrics aligned with currently selected curve mode. The dashboard displays one card per active channel — the visible card (e.g. Commercial Rep Visits) is representative; all channels follow the same pattern showing Current Spend, Response, ROI, and Saturation.
rows.formula: Current ROI_i = Current Response_i / Current Spend_i; Headroom_i = 1 - Saturation_i.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: strong current ROI with meaningful headroom. Risk: high spend with low marginal gain.
rows.decision_supported: Immediate shortlist for increase, hold, or cut decisions.
rows.recommended_next_action: Carry shortlisted channels into allocator constraints.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Comparing channels without considering confidence interval width.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Values are model-based summaries at current spend.

rows.title: Marginal ROI View
rows.business_question: What does the next unit of spend likely return right now?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Evaluate diminishing returns and decide where incremental budget still has headroom.
rows.why_this_exists: What does the next unit of spend likely return right now?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Slope view of response curve showing marginal return.
rows.formula: mROI ≈ ΔResponse/ΔSpend around current spend.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: mROI above strategic hurdle rate. Risk: mROI below hurdle indicates inefficient increment.
rows.decision_supported: Which channel gets the next incremental budget dollar/euro.
rows.recommended_next_action: Set or confirm channel-level stop/go thresholds using mROI ranking.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Confusing average ROI with marginal ROI.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Locally estimated slope can be noisy where curve points are sparse.

rows.title: Saturation View
rows.business_question: How close is each channel to its practical ceiling?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Evaluate diminishing returns and decide where incremental budget still has headroom.
rows.why_this_exists: How close is each channel to its practical ceiling?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Normalized curve expressing distance to channel ceiling.
rows.formula: Saturation% = Response(x) / MaxResponse × 100.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: lower saturation indicates growth headroom. Risk: high saturation suggests diminishing returns.
rows.decision_supported: Whether to scale a channel or hold/reduce incremental spend.
rows.recommended_next_action: Reallocate from >70% saturation channels to lower-saturation channels if mROI also supports it.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Assuming identical saturation thresholds across all channel types.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Derived from modelled curve ceiling, not a hard operational cap.

rows.title: Scenario Builder (Optimize)
rows.business_question: What constraints and objective should shape the optimized plan?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Turn strategy constraints into a recommended allocation and compare business impact.
rows.why_this_exists: What constraints and objective should shape the optimized plan?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Inputs for objective, budget band, and channel-level min/max constraints.
rows.formula: max Σ Response_i(Spend_i) subject to Σ Spend_i = Budget and Min_i ≤ Spend_i ≤ Max_i.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: constraints reflect real business guardrails. Risk: unrealistic bounds force poor allocations.
rows.decision_supported: Formal optimization setup before running scenario.
rows.recommended_next_action: Set realistic min/max by channel, then run and inspect optimization metrics.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Treating optimizer output as valid when constraints are unrealistic.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Output quality depends on curve quality and constraint realism.

rows.title: Optimization Delta Table
rows.business_question: Is the recommended allocation materially better than current?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Turn strategy constraints into a recommended allocation and compare business impact.
rows.why_this_exists: Does the optimized scenario materially improve outcomes vs current plan?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Current vs optimized totals and deltas for spend, incremental sales, profit, and ROI.
rows.formula: ROI = Incremental/Spend; Profit = Incremental - Spend; ΔMetric%=(Optimized-Current)/Current×100.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: positive incremental and profit deltas with acceptable risk. Risk: ROI gains driven by unrealistic cuts or noisy channels.
rows.decision_supported: Go/no-go on moving forward with this scenario.
rows.recommended_next_action: If uplift is material, export and validate via simulator sensitivity checks.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Overweighting ROI without checking absolute outcome changes.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: For what-if mode, server curve interpolation drives estimates.
rows.worked_example: Example: current 2.0M incremental on 1.4M spend gives 1.43x ROI; optimized 2.3M on 1.5M gives 1.53x ROI, uplift +0.10x.

rows.title: Scenario Results Table
rows.business_question: Which specific channels increase or decrease in the optimized plan?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Turn strategy constraints into a recommended allocation and compare business impact.
rows.why_this_exists: Which specific channels increase or decrease in the optimized plan?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Row-level channel deltas between current and optimized spends/shares.
rows.formula: Delta_i = Optimized_i - Current_i; Delta%_i = Delta_i / Current_i × 100.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: reallocations align with curve headroom and business guardrails. Risk: large swings in uncertain channels.
rows.decision_supported: Which channel-level moves to operationalize.
rows.recommended_next_action: Flag large positive/negative deltas for commercial team validation.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Assuming all channel deltas are equally executable.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Execution feasibility is external to model outputs.

rows.title: Budget Split Chart
rows.business_question: How does spend share shift between current and optimized allocation?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Turn strategy constraints into a recommended allocation and compare business impact.
rows.why_this_exists: How does spend share shift between current and optimized allocation?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Visual side-by-side of current and optimized channel budget shares.
rows.formula: Share_i = Spend_i / Σ Spend_j (computed for current and optimized plans).
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: share moves are strategic and explainable. Risk: extreme concentration shifts.
rows.decision_supported: Communication readiness of reallocation narrative.
rows.recommended_next_action: Use this chart to present high-level reallocation story to stakeholders.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Reading share change without absolute spend context.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Share visuals mask absolute budget size changes.

rows.title: Planned Scenario Panel
rows.business_question: Is this scenario packaged and ready to transfer to simulation/approval?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Turn strategy constraints into a recommended allocation and compare business impact.
rows.why_this_exists: Is this scenario packaged and ready to transfer to simulation/approval?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Scenario naming, save/export, and transfer actions.
rows.formula: No KPI formula; operational packaging step.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: scenario labeled clearly and saved. Risk: ambiguous scenario naming causes misuse.
rows.decision_supported: Whether scenario is governance-ready.
rows.recommended_next_action: Save with explicit objective and date context, then open in Simulator.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Confusing draft and approved scenarios.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Scenario snapshot may drift if rerun with different model version.

rows.title: Scenario Builder (What-If)
rows.business_question: What happens if I manually nudge channel spends up or down?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Turn strategy constraints into a recommended allocation and compare business impact.
rows.why_this_exists: What happens if I manually nudge channel spends up or down?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Manual adjustment controls with real-time impact estimation.
rows.formula: NewSpend_i = CurrentSpend_i × (1 + Adj%_i); NewResponse = Σ Response_i(NewSpend_i).
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: scenario remains beneficial under realistic manual perturbations. Risk: fragile scenario collapses under small changes.
rows.decision_supported: Sensitivity of recommended plan.
rows.recommended_next_action: Stress high-risk channels before approval.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Treating what-if as optimization output.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: What-if uses current model state and may change with updated curves.

rows.title: Simulator Header & Saved Scenarios
rows.business_question: Which scenario baseline am I currently testing?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Stress-test plan assumptions with manual multipliers before committing spend changes.
rows.why_this_exists: Which scenario baseline am I currently testing?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Scenario identity, save/load controls, and metadata context.
rows.formula: No KPI formula; context control.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: clear scenario provenance. Risk: evaluating wrong loaded scenario.
rows.decision_supported: Scope and provenance control for simulation runs.
rows.recommended_next_action: Confirm scenario name/version before interpreting any metric deltas.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Assuming all scenarios share identical objective and constraints.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Saved scenarios persist and can become outdated.

rows.title: Channel Multipliers Panel
rows.business_question: How do manual multiplier changes alter expected outcome by channel?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Stress-test plan assumptions with manual multipliers before committing spend changes.
rows.why_this_exists: How do manual multiplier changes alter expected outcome by channel?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Slider-based multipliers applied to baseline spend by channel.
rows.formula: ManualSpend_i = BaselineSpend_i × Multiplier_i.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: moderate moves reveal robust direction. Risk: extreme multipliers outside feasible operations.
rows.decision_supported: Sensitivity of channel recommendations.
rows.recommended_next_action: Test downside and upside bands for top reallocated channels.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Ignoring execution limits when using high multipliers.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Applies to current loaded scenario only.

rows.title: Simulator Outcome Metrics
rows.business_question: Do manual assumptions preserve value uplift?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Stress-test plan assumptions with manual multipliers before committing spend changes.
rows.why_this_exists: Does the optimized scenario materially improve outcomes vs current plan?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Current vs optimized totals and deltas for spend, incremental sales, profit, and ROI.
rows.formula: ROI = Incremental/Spend; Profit = Incremental - Spend; ΔMetric%=(Optimized-Current)/Current×100.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: positive incremental and profit deltas with acceptable risk. Risk: ROI gains driven by unrealistic cuts or noisy channels.
rows.decision_supported: Go/no-go on moving forward with this scenario.
rows.recommended_next_action: If uplift is material, export and validate via simulator sensitivity checks.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Overweighting ROI without checking absolute outcome changes.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: For what-if mode, server curve interpolation drives estimates.
rows.worked_example: Example: baseline volume 100k plus summed curve deltas +8k yields simulated 108k volume (+8%).

rows.title: Insight Headline & Trust
rows.business_question: What is the headline recommendation and how much should I trust it?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Translate model outputs into recommendations, risks, and confidence for decision meetings.
rows.why_this_exists: What is the headline recommendation and how much should I trust it?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Executive summary panel with top performer and trust indicators.
rows.formula: Trust/grade is computed server-side from model diagnostics and uncertainty signals.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: strong trust with coherent channel story. Risk: weak trust with aggressive recommendation.
rows.decision_supported: Whether to act immediately or demand additional validation.
rows.recommended_next_action: If trust is weak, review diagnostics and run conservative pilot.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Taking headline recommendation without confidence context.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Trust available only when PyMC artifacts are present.

rows.title: Channel Radar
rows.business_question: How do channels compare across multiple dimensions at once?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Translate model outputs into recommendations, risks, and confidence for decision meetings.
rows.why_this_exists: How do channels compare across multiple dimensions at once?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Radar profile combining ROI, confidence/headroom/adstock proxies.
rows.formula: Axis scores are normalized to 0..100 per metric for comparability.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: balanced, consistently strong shape. Risk: spiky profile with weak confidence/headroom.
rows.decision_supported: Balanced portfolio-style channel selection.
rows.recommended_next_action: Use radar to avoid over-optimizing on one metric only.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Interpreting area size as absolute value without axis context.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Metric set differs between PyMC and base mode.

rows.title: Efficiency Frontier
rows.business_question: Which channels lie on the efficient spend-vs-return frontier?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Translate model outputs into recommendations, risks, and confidence for decision meetings.
rows.why_this_exists: Which channels lie on the efficient spend-vs-return frontier?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Scatter of channel spend versus ROI to identify efficient/inefficient positions.
rows.formula: Each point: (x=Spend_i, y=ROI_i). Frontier channels maximize ROI for given spend.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: scaled channels remain near frontier. Risk: heavy spend in low-ROI area.
rows.decision_supported: Reallocation from inefficient to efficient channels.
rows.recommended_next_action: Target channels below frontier for corrective action.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Ignoring confidence/CI while interpreting frontier.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Frontier interpretation should be paired with uncertainty checks.

rows.title: Adstock / Carryover
rows.business_question: Which channels create delayed impact and how long does it persist?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Translate model outputs into recommendations, risks, and confidence for decision meetings.
rows.why_this_exists: Which channels create delayed impact and how long does it persist?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Carryover characteristics from adstock analysis.
rows.formula: Half-life (periods) derived from channel adstock decay parameters (server-computed).
rows.unit_and_scale: Time periods and normalized carryover indicators.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: carryover assumptions align with known channel behavior. Risk: mismatch implies model misspecification.
rows.decision_supported: Timing and pacing decisions for campaigns.
rows.recommended_next_action: Adjust pacing for channels with long carryover to avoid over-frequency.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Assuming carryover means immediate performance.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Available only with PyMC adstock outputs.

rows.title: Recommendations
rows.business_question: What concrete business action is suggested from current model evidence?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Translate model outputs into recommendations, risks, and confidence for decision meetings.
rows.why_this_exists: What concrete business action is suggested from current model evidence?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Action recommendations generated from model outputs and constraints.
rows.formula: No single formula; recommendation is a rule-based synthesis of ROI, saturation, and diagnostics.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: recommendation aligns with tab evidence. Risk: recommendation conflicts with weak trust signals.
rows.decision_supported: Action commitment and owner alignment.
rows.recommended_next_action: Translate recommendation into a scenario and validate in Simulator.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Treating recommendation as deterministic truth.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Recommendation quality depends on artifact completeness.

rows.title: Diagnostics
rows.business_question: Do diagnostics reveal reasons to limit confidence in the recommendation?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Translate model outputs into recommendations, risks, and confidence for decision meetings.
rows.why_this_exists: Do diagnostics reveal reasons to limit confidence in the recommendation?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Diagnostic checks for model health and anomalies.
rows.formula: Diagnostic metrics are computed server-side from model fit and uncertainty outputs.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: no severe flags. Risk: material warnings that invalidate aggressive changes.
rows.decision_supported: Confidence gating before operational rollout.
rows.recommended_next_action: If red flags appear, run conservative pilot and revisit model.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Ignoring diagnostics when headline upside looks attractive.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Diagnostics can differ by engine/tier/version.

rows.title: Configuration Selection
rows.business_question: Am I evaluating the correct market/product/version/tier before making decisions?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Control the active model context and verify model quality before operational decisions.
rows.why_this_exists: Am I evaluating the correct market/product/version/tier before making decisions?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Selectors defining active analytical context and tier availability.
rows.formula: No KPI formula; selection determines all downstream data queries.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: context matches intended decision scope. Risk: wrong version/tier selected.
rows.decision_supported: Scope and validity of all downstream analysis.
rows.recommended_next_action: Lock selection before sharing outputs externally.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Comparing outputs from mismatched versions.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Latest may move as new runs publish.

rows.title: Model Quality One-Pager
rows.business_question: Is this model fit for operational use?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Control the active model context and verify model quality before operational decisions.
rows.why_this_exists: Does this model clear quality thresholds for business use?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Core quality metrics summarizing fit and error.
rows.formula: R² = 1 - SSE/SST; MAPE = mean(|Actual-Forecast|/Actual).
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: high R² with controlled MAPE. Risk: low R² or high MAPE undermines planning confidence.
rows.decision_supported: Model go/no-go for operational planning.
rows.recommended_next_action: If below threshold, prefer conservative changes or alternate version.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Assuming high R² guarantees robust channel-level allocation.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Out-of-sample diagnostics should also be considered when available.
rows.worked_example: Example: Actual=100, Forecast=92 gives abs pct error 8%; aggregate these for MAPE.

rows.title: Efficiency Chart
rows.business_question: Which channels over- or under-deliver relative to spend share?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Control the active model context and verify model quality before operational decisions.
rows.why_this_exists: Which channels over- or under-deliver relative to spend share?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Compares spend share with effect share by channel.
rows.formula: Efficiency Ratio_i = EffectShare_i / SpendShare_i.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: ratio >1 for priority channels. Risk: heavy channels with ratio <1.
rows.decision_supported: Identify over-invested and under-invested channels.
rows.recommended_next_action: Rebalance budget from persistently <1.0 channels toward >1.0 channels with headroom.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Ignoring minimum strategic spend requirements.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Effect share is model-derived, not direct causal proof.

rows.title: Driver Contribution
rows.business_question: What proportion of outcome is explained by each driver category?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Control the active model context and verify model quality before operational decisions.
rows.why_this_exists: What proportion of outcome is explained by each driver category?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Contribution breakdown across media and non-media drivers.
rows.formula: DriverShare_d = Contribution_d / Σ Contributions.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: media contribution level is plausible and stable. Risk: implausible dominance by one driver category.
rows.decision_supported: Balance between media actions and contextual/non-media levers.
rows.recommended_next_action: Investigate any large driver shift before committing big allocation changes.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Interpreting contextual drivers as directly controllable media levers.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Driver grouping rules can vary by model configuration.

rows.title: Territory Map
rows.business_question: Which territories show strongest or weakest modeled effects?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Identify territorial heterogeneity so deployment can be tailored by region and tier.
rows.why_this_exists: Which territories show strongest or weakest modeled effects?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Choropleth/heatmap of territorial effect intensity.
rows.formula: TerritoryEffect_t = Σ channel effects in territory t (hierarchical model output).
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: regional differences are interpretable with market context. Risk: noisy territory outliers drive overreaction.
rows.decision_supported: Regional targeting and budget localization strategy.
rows.recommended_next_action: Use map outliers to prioritize deeper territory review.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Treating color intensity as absolute sales magnitude.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Available only in advanced tier with territory artifacts.

rows.title: Territory Detail Table
rows.business_question: How do territories compare on channel-specific effect metrics?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Identify territorial heterogeneity so deployment can be tailored by region and tier.
rows.why_this_exists: How do territories compare on channel-specific effect metrics?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Sortable table of territory-level metrics and channel effects.
rows.formula: Rank by TerritoryEffect_t or selected channel beta/impact metric.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: pattern supports differentiated execution. Risk: random-like rank instability.
rows.decision_supported: Where to tailor channel intensity by territory.
rows.recommended_next_action: Build regional action list from top/bottom territory clusters.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Overfitting field actions to small modeled differences.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Territory granularity may mask local heterogeneity.

rows.title: Channel Dispersion
rows.business_question: How uneven is channel effect across territories?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Identify territorial heterogeneity so deployment can be tailored by region and tier.
rows.why_this_exists: How uneven is channel effect across territories?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Cards summarizing channel variability across territories.
rows.formula: Dispersion_i = (max_i - min_i) / max_i × 100.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: manageable dispersion for nationally standardized channels. Risk: high dispersion suggests local strategy needed.
rows.decision_supported: National vs territory-tailored channel strategy.
rows.recommended_next_action: High-dispersion channels should get region-specific planning.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Interpreting dispersion as certainty rather than variability.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Sensitive to territory sample coverage.

rows.title: Advanced Tier Lock
rows.business_question: Why is territory analysis unavailable and what unlocks it?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Identify territorial heterogeneity so deployment can be tailored by region and tier.
rows.why_this_exists: Why is territory analysis unavailable and what unlocks it?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Access gate explaining advanced tier requirement.
rows.formula: No KPI; capability gating based on selected model tier.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: users clearly understand unlock path. Risk: misinterpreting lock as data failure.
rows.decision_supported: Whether to switch tier before geography analysis.
rows.recommended_next_action: Go to Settings and switch to Advanced tier when available.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Raising data-quality incidents for an intentional tier gate.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Advanced tier availability is product/version-specific.

rows.title: Tab Orientation
rows.business_question: What is this tab for, and which decision should I make here?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Orient business users on current spend, return, and where to investigate next.
rows.why_this_exists: What is this tab for, and which decision should I make here?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Landing context for the tab, including headline and action orientation.
rows.formula: No arithmetic KPI. This section is contextual and directional.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: context and selected scope are clearly aligned. Risk: users act on wrong product/year context.
rows.decision_supported: Should I continue analysis in this tab or switch to another module?
rows.recommended_next_action: Verify selected context, then use the main KPI/graph below for decisioning.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Treating overview text as evidence rather than using quantitative sections.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Depends on selected model version and available artifacts.

rows.title: Dashboard Context Selectors
rows.business_question: Am I looking at the right country, product, and time period?
rows.tab_business_purpose: Orient business users on current spend, return, and where to investigate next.
rows.why_this_exists: Am I looking at the right country, product, and time period?
rows.kpi_or_chart_definition: Country, product, and year dropdowns plus theme toggle controlling the entire dashboard scope.
rows.formula: No direct formula. Selection determines all downstream API queries and data context.
rows.how_to_read_good_vs_risk: Good: selectors match intended analysis scope. Risk: wrong context selected leads to misinterpreted results.
rows.decision_supported: Ensuring all subsequent analysis applies to the correct market, product, and time period.
rows.recommended_next_action: Verify country/product/year before interpreting any dashboard data. Use theme toggle for presentation preferences.
rows.misinterpretation_risks: Assuming results apply to a different product or time period than selected.
rows.data_freshness_or_caveats: Available options depend on which models and data have been loaded into the system.
